response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with ~14 years of experience and meets the BS + 8 years educational path, but the role is a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerator hardware. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture analysis, SIMD/MIMD work, or hardware-software co-design at the silicon level. The closest adjacencies—C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, and robotic-arm work—are tangential and outdated relative to this specialized hardware-architecture mandate. The skill profile is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements.
strengths: Deep, broad software engineering experience including C/C++ and low-latency, high-throughput systems
Strong fundamentals in performance-sensitive work (async I/O, caching, fibers, millions of req/s)
Demonstrated learning agility, mentorship, and open-source/community contribution
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator, or DNN training/inference optimization experience
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware-software co-design background
Career is web/mobile/backend focused; lacks the silicon-level performance-architecture depth this Principal IC role requires
